特斯拉（TSLA）正在推出自己的太阳能逆变器，该逆变器将与Powerwall一起完善公司的家用太阳能产品。
   自2016年收购SolarCity以来，特斯拉一直在提供自己的太阳能解决方案。
 埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）精心策划收购的策略之一是让特斯拉（Tesla）通过不同的产品在市场中脱颖而出，这些产品已成为太阳能行业中的某种商品。
 该汽车制造商试图制造自己的太阳能电池板，但没有成功。
 但是，特斯拉的Powerwall家用电池组与住宅太阳能装置非常匹配，因此已经取得了成功，并且太阳能屋顶瓦也开始取得一些成功。
 出乎意料的是，考虑到特斯拉在电动汽车的电力电子方面的广泛专业知识，该公司从未拥有过自己的太阳能逆变器，而太阳能逆变器是太阳能装置的重要组成部分……直到现在。
 现在，该公司正在为其产品阵容添加新的内部设计的“特斯拉太阳能逆变器”： 
“特斯拉太阳能逆变器完善了特斯拉的家用太阳能系统，将直流电从太阳能转换为交流电以供家庭消费。特斯拉在电力电子领域享有盛名的专业知识已与强大的安全功能和简单的安装过程相结合，以生产出与Solar Roof和传统太阳能电池板兼容的出色太阳能逆变器。安装后，房主可以使用Tesla应用程序管理其太阳能系统并监控能耗，从而带来真正独特的生态系统体验。”
 根据新买家的说法，新逆变器已经与特斯拉太阳能装置一起安装。您可以访问我们的Tesla太阳能指南，以了解有关Tesla屋顶太阳能电池板安装和太阳能屋顶瓦片的更多信息。
    尺寸26英寸x 16英寸x 6英寸660毫米x 411毫米x 158毫米
  这是一个不足为奇的发展。最令人惊讶的是，特斯拉在电力电子领域拥有广泛的专业知识，这是前所未有的。
   在拥有自己的太阳能逆变器之前，特斯拉已经使用Delta和SolarEdge逆变器。
 许多人认为，太阳能逆变器的最重要特征是效率，其效率为97.5％，比达美航空的97％略好，但不及SolarEdge的99％效率。
 就是说，特斯拉太阳能逆变器有一些不错的功能，例如4点跟踪器，如果您的太阳能模块来自屋顶不同部分的不同串，则这些功能很有用-太阳能屋顶很可能具有某些功能瓷砖安装。 
Tesla太阳能逆变器的另一大优点是无线更新功能，这意味着该公司可以随着时间的推移改进产品的软件。  最后，最大的优势（尽管不确定）是潜在的成本。 特斯拉可以利用其已经足够大的电动汽车和充电站的电力电子生产能力，来获得比竞争对手更好的成本。  您正在阅读Electrek，这是专家，他们日复一日地发布有关特斯拉，电动汽车和绿色能源的新闻。 请务必查看我们的主页以获取所有最新新闻，并在Twitter，Facebook和LinkedIn上关注Electrek以保持关注。 不知道从哪里开始？ 查看我们的YouTube频道以获取最新评论。
note_en:
Tesla (TSLA) is launching its own solar power inverter, which completes the company’s home solar offering along with the Powerwall.
   Tesla has been offering its own solar power solution since the acquisition of SolarCity back in 2016.
 One of Elon Musk’s strategies when master-minding the acquisition was for Tesla to differentiate itself in the market with different products, which have become somewhat of commodities in the solar industry.
 The automaker tried to make its own solar panels, but it didn’t work out.
 However, Tesla has had success with its Powerwall, a home battery pack that matches well with a residential solar installation, and it is starting to have some success with its  solar roof tiles.
 Surprisingly, considering Tesla’s extensive expertise in power electronics in its electric vehicles, the company never had its own solar power inverter, an important part of a solar installation… until now.
 Now the company is adding a new in-house designed ‘Tesla Solar Inverter’ to its lineup:
   “Tesla Solar Inverter completes the Tesla home solar system, converting DC power from solar to AC power for home consumption. Tesla’s renowned expertise in power electronics has been combined with robust safety features and a simple installation process to produce an outstanding solar inverter that is compatible with both Solar Roof and traditional solar panels. Once installed, homeowners use the Tesla app to manage their solar system and monitor energy consumption, resulting in a truly unique ecosystem experience.”
 The new inverter is already being installed with new Tesla solar installations according to new buyers. You can visit our  Tesla solar guide for more information about Tesla rooftop solar panel installations and solar roof tiles.
    Size 26″ x 16″ x 6″ 660 mm x 411 mm x 158 mm
  This is an unsurprising development. The most surprising thing is that it didn’t happen before with Tesla’s extensive expertise in power electronics.
   Prior to having its own solar inverter, Tesla has been known to use Delta and SolarEdge inverters.
 Many argue that the most important feature of a solar inverter is its efficiency and at 97.5%, it’s a bit better than Delta’s 97% but not as good as SolarEdge’s 99% efficiency.
 That said, there are some nice of features to the Tesla Solar Inverter, like 4 point trackers, which can be useful if your solar modules are coming from different strings on different parts of your roof – something that can be very likely with a solar roof tile installation.
 Another great thing about the Tesla Solar Inverter is the over-the-air update capability, which means the company could improve on the product’s software over time.
 Finally, the greatest advantage (although it’s uncertain) is potentially the cost. Tesla could potentially be able to achieve a better cost than the competition by leveraging its already large power electronic production capacity for its electric vehicles and charging stations.
  You’re reading Electrek— experts who break news about  Tesla,  electric vehicles, and  green energy, day after day. Be sure to check out our  homepage for all the latest news, and follow Electrek on  Twitter,  Facebook, and  LinkedIn to stay in the loop. Don’t know where to start? Check out our  YouTube channel for the latest reviews.
title: 特斯拉（TSLA）推出自己的太阳能逆变器
title_en: Tesla (TSLA) launches its own solar power inverter

美国检察官办公室周四宣布，丰田汽车将因长期违反《清洁空气法》而被罚款1.8亿美元，这是有史以来最大的民事处罚，是违反联邦排放报告要求的。
 根据在曼哈顿提起的投诉，这家全球汽车制造商大约在2005年至2015年间没有系统地报告有碍其汽车控制排气管排放的缺陷，违反了旨在保护公众健康和环境免受有害空气污染物污染的标准。
 检察官办公室说，日本的丰田管理人员和工作人员知道这种做法，但未能阻止这种做法，这家汽车制造商很可能售出了数百万辆存在缺陷的车辆。
 美国代理律师奥黛丽·史特劳斯（Audrey Strauss）在一份声明中说：“丰田公司对违规行为视而不见。”丰田已同意不接受罚款。
 该汽车制造商发言人埃里克·布斯（Eric Booth）表示，一旦失误曝光，该公司已立即向有关部门发出警报，报告延迟“造成的排放影响可忽略不计”。
 布斯补充说：“尽管如此，我们认识到我们的某些报告协议没有达到我们自己的高标准，我们很高兴解决了这一问题。”
  丰田汽车是仅次于大众汽车的全球第二大汽车制造商，并且曾经凭借其最畅销的普锐斯（Prius）汽油-电动混合动力乘用车赢得了清洁技术的声誉。但是，这家汽车巨头在2019年决定支持特朗普政府降低尾气排放标准，再加上相对缓慢的全电动汽车的推出，使其成为环保组织的批评目标。 
丰田汽车最近的车型阵容中大量使用耗油量很大的运动型多功能车，它们的价格标签要大得多，并带来更高的利润率。根据美国环境保护署（EPA）的最新报告，丰田汽车的燃油效率是该行业中最差的，导致美国乘用车和卡车的行驶里程和污染总体上出现了五年来的首次恶化。
 现在，许多汽车制造商正准备迎接即将上任的拜登政府可能采取的措施，以恢复更严格的排气管排放规定，并表明他们致力于与政府官员合作。
 “令人震惊的是，汽车公司欺骗污染法规，然后希望拜登总统与他们就新的清洁汽车标准进行谈判，”环保组织生物多样性中心负责安全气候运输运动的丹·贝克尔说。 “在履行了先前的承诺后，为什么有人应该信任汽车制造商？”
 近年来，汽车行业一直受到与排放有关的丑闻的困扰。 2017年，大众汽车承认美国政府已经操纵其柴油动力汽车以在测试过程中达到空气质量标准，即使该汽车在常规驾驶中超过了这些标准，大众汽车也承认其密谋欺骗美国政府。去年，另一家德国汽车制造商戴姆勒（Daimler）同意支付22亿美元，以了结关于在美国销售的梅赛德斯·奔驰汽车和货车被编程为欺骗排放测试的指控。
 车主本身也被指控篡改车辆。今年的一份联邦报告得出结论，在过去十年中，超过半百万辆柴油皮卡车的拥有者和经营者一直在非法禁用其车辆中的排放控制技术，从而造成相当于900万辆额外卡车的超额排放。
 仍然严重依赖化石燃料的交通运输构成了全球变暖排放量的最大组成部分，超过了电力，制造业或农业产生的排放量。科学家长期以来一直警告说，世界上的汽车和卡车必须远离汽油，以避免气候变化的最严重影响。
 最新估计显示，到2020年，与美国交通相关的排放确实下降了近15％，原因是成千上万的人停止开车去上班，而航空公司取消了航班。但是专家警告说，除非政策制定者采取更强有力的行动保持低排放，否则汽车和卡车的排放将反弹。
note_en:
Toyota Motor is set to pay a $180 million fine for longstanding violations of the Clean Air Act, the U.S. attorney’s Office in Manhattan announced on Thursday, the largest civil penalty ever levied for a breach of federal emissions-reporting requirements.
 From about 2005 to 2015, the global automaker systematically failed to report defects that interfered with how its cars controlled tailpipe emissions, violating standards designed to protect public health and the environment from harmful air pollutants, according to a complaint filed in Manhattan.
 Toyota managers and staff in Japan knew about the practice but failed to stop it, and the automaker quite likely sold millions of vehicles with the defects, the attorney’s office said.
 “Toyota shut its eyes to the noncompliance,” Audrey Strauss, the acting U.S. attorney, said in a statement. Toyota has agreed not to contest the fine.
 Eric Booth, a spokesman for the automaker, said that the company had alerted the authorities as soon as the lapses came to light, and that the delay in reporting “resulted in a negligible emissions impact, if any.”
 “Nonetheless, we recognize that some of our reporting protocols fell short of our own high standards, and we are pleased to have resolved this matter,” Mr. Booth added.
  Toyota is the world’s second-largest automaker behind Volkswagen, and once built a reputation for clean technology on the back of its best-selling Prius gasoline-electric hybrid passengers cars. But the auto giant’s decision in 2019 to  support the Trump administration’s rollback of tailpipe emissions standards — coupled with its relatively slow introduction of fully-electric vehicles — has made it a target of criticism from environmental groups.
 Toyota’s more recent lineup of models has been heavy on gas-guzzling sports-utility vehicles, which come with far bigger price tags and have brought far higher profit margins. According to  a recent report from the Environmental Protection Agency, Toyota vehicles delivered some of the worst fuel efficiency in the industry, leading to an overall worsening of mileage and pollution from passenger cars and trucks in the United States for the first time in five years.
 Many automakers are now bracing for a likely push by the incoming Biden administration for a return to stricter tailpipe emissions rules, and have signaled they are committed to working with administration officials.
 “It’s appalling that auto companies cheat on pollution rules but then want President Biden to negotiate with them about new clean car standards,” said Dan Becker, who directs the Safe Climate Transport Campaign at the Center for Biological Diversity, an environmental group. “After reneging on their previous commitments, why should anyone trust the automakers?”
 The auto industry has been plagued by emissions-related scandals in recent years. In 2017, Volkswagen pleaded guilty to conspiring to defraud the United States government after it acknowledged that it had  rigged its diesel-powered cars to meet air-quality standards while being tested, even though the cars exceeded those standards in regular driving. Last year, Daimler, another German automaker,  agreed to pay $2.2 billion to settle accusations that Mercedes-Benz cars and vans sold in the United States were programmed to cheat on emissions tests.
 Car owners themselves have also been accused of tampering with their vehicles.  A federal report concluded this year that owners and operators of more than half a million diesel pickup trucks have been illegally disabling the emissions control technology in their vehicles over the past decade, allowing excess emissions equivalent to 9 million extra trucks on the road.
 Transportation, which remains heavily dependent on fossil fuels,  makes up the biggest chunk of emissions of planet-warming emissions, ahead of emissions generated by the power sector, manufacturing or agriculture. Scientists have long warned that the world’s cars and trucks must shift away from gasoline to avoid the worst effects of  climate change.
 Recent estimates have shown that transportation-related emissions in the United States did decline almost 15 percent in 2020, as millions of people stopped driving to work and airlines canceled flights. But experts warn that emissions from cars and trucks will rebound unless policymakers take stronger action to keep emissions low.
title: 丰田因违反《清洁空气法》而被处以数十年罚款
title_en: Toyota to Pay a Record Fine for a Decade of Clean Air Act Violations

据知情人士透露，Postmates X是Uber去年以26.5亿美元收购的按需交付创业公司的机器人部门，它正在寻求投资者成为一家独立公司。
 这家初创公司被称为Serve Robotics，这是对由Postmates X开发和驾驶的黄色和黑色装饰的自动人行道送货机器人的致敬。Serve机器人将与Pink Dot Stores合作在西好莱坞进行送货。可能是新创业公司的核心。
   根据将要购买给投资者的交易，该公司将由阿里·卡沙尼（Ali Kashani）经营，后者领导Postmates X并领导服务计划。 Anthony Armenta将负责该初创公司的软件工作，而Aaron Leiba将负责硬件-保持与Postmates X相同的职位。
 优步将保留Serve Robotics的所有权，并与该初创公司保持商业协议。服务将交换IP和资产。一位知情人士透露，Uber正在讨论保留这家新公司约25％的股份。
 到目前为止，还没有一个名为Serve Robotics的法律实体。但是，一个网站域serverobotics.com已于1月6日注册。
  此次分拆将与优步简化的业务战略相吻合。优步的简化业务战略在2019年5月公开市场首次亮相后就开始形成，并在去年因COVID-19大流行给乘车公司施加压力而加速。两年前，Uber的企业遍及整个交通领域，从叫车和微型交通到物流，公共交通，食品配送以及诸如自动驾驶汽车和出租车的未来派赌注。首席执行官达拉·科斯罗萨西（Dara Khosrowshahi）在推动公司实现盈利的同时，捣毁了一切，但只不过是厨房洗手台。
 2020年，Uber与Lime达成了一项复杂的交易，卸下了共享的踏板车和自行车部门Jump，出售了价值5亿美元的物流业务，剥离了Uber Freight，并摆脱了其自动驾驶汽车部门Uber ATG和空中出租车业务Uber Elevate。 
Aurora以与Jump-Lime交易类似的交易收购了Uber ATG。 Aurora没有为Uber ATG支付现金。相反，Uber交出了其在ATG的股权，并向Aurora投资了4亿美元，这使其获得了合并后公司的26％的股份，
  交付仍然是Uber投资的领域。随着对Uber Eats交付服务的需求激增，该公司看到了一个机会，因此开始寻求收购以增强其地位。 Uber尝试收购Grubhub失败，但失败了，输给了欧洲重量级的Just Eat Takeaway。
 Uber登陆Postmates，并于2020年7月同意以价值26.5亿美元的全股票交易方式收购这家交付初创公司。交易于十二月完成。
  在公司悄悄收购了Kashani的初创公司Lox Inc之后，Postmates对人行道送货机器人的探索始于2017年。作为公司研发部门Postmates X的负责人，Kashani着手回答这个问题：“为什么要移动两磅的墨西哥卷饼有两吨的汽车？
 Postmates于2018年12月发布了其首款Serve自主交付机器人。第二代产品具有相同的设计，但激光雷达传感器却有所不同，并且几乎没有其他升级，于2019年夏季问世，计划在洛杉矶进行商业发布。
  根据Kashani在10月的TC会议：Mobility 2020活动中发表的评论，Postmates没有与合作伙伴合作，而是使用自己的交付数据构成了如何设计和部署人行道机器人的基础。
 “当您查看数据时，发现超过一半的交付都在短距离之内，这变得毫不费力-这些机器人实际上可以完成它们，” Kashani在谈到自主交付机器人在交付方面的应用时说道。 
Postmates X使用公司的历史交付数据来开发模拟，然后将其用于Serve机器人的设计中。 它帮助团队确定了所需的电池寿命以及货舱的大小以及其他功能。  该机器人仅代表Postmates的一小部分送货业务。 但是，由于COVID-19推动了对非接触式交付的需求，该公司对它在商业化运营的两个城市洛杉矶和旧金山的机器人的兴趣有所增加。  卡沙尼（Kashani）早在10月就指出，这些机器人已经在洛杉矶完成了数千次送货，并正准备扩展到该市的西好莱坞飞地。 这种扩张是在去年下半年开始的。 服务机器人已更改为亮粉红色，以匹配粉红色点商店的标志性颜色。
note_en:
Postmates X, the robotics division of the on-demand delivery startup that  Uber acquired last year for $2.65 billion, is seeking investors in its bid to become a separate company, according to several people familiar with the plans.
 The startup is being referred to as Serve Robotics, a nod to the yellow and black-emblazoned autonomous sidewalk delivery bot that was developed and piloted by Postmates X. The Serve robot, which recently partnered with Pink Dot Stores for deliveries in West Hollywood, will likely be the centerpiece of the new startup.
   Under the deal, which is being shopped to investors, the company would be run by Ali Kashani, who heads up Postmates X and leads the Serve program. Anthony Armenta would lead the startup’s software efforts and Aaron Leiba would be in charge of hardware — keeping the same positions they hold at Postmates X.
 Uber would retain an ownership stake in Serve Robotics and maintain a commercial agreement with the startup. Serve would get the IP and assets in exchange. Uber is in discussions to retain about a 25% stake in the new startup, according to one source familiar with the deal.
 There is not a legal entity — as of yet — named Serve Robotics. However, a website domain serverobotics.com was registered January 6.
  The spinoff would be in line with Uber’s streamlined business strategy that began to take shape after its public market debut in May 2019 and accelerated last year as the COVID-19 pandemic put pressure on the ride-hailing company. Two years ago, Uber had enterprises across the transportation landscape from ride-hailing and micromobility to logistics, public transit, food delivery and futuristic bets like autonomous vehicles and air taxis. CEO Dara Khosrowshahi has dismantled the everything-but-the-kitchen-sink approach as he pushes the company towards profitability.
 In 2020, Uber offloaded shared scooter and bike unit Jump in a complex deal with Lime, sold a  stake worth $500 million in its logistics spin off Uber Freight and rid itself of its autonomous vehicle unit Uber ATG and its air taxi play Uber Elevate.
 Aurora  acquired Uber ATG in a deal that had a similar structure to the Jump-Lime transaction. Aurora didn’t pay cash for Uber ATG. Instead, Uber handed over its equity in ATG and invested $400 million into Aurora, which gave it a 26% stake in the combined company,
  Delivery remained the one area that Uber has invested in. The company, seeing an opportunity as demand skyrocketed for its Uber Eats delivery service, started looking for an acquisition to strengthen its position. Uber tried and failed to buy Grubhub, losing out to European heavyweight Just Eat Takeaway.
 Uber landed on Postmates and in July 2020 agreed to buy the delivery startup in an all-stock deal valued at $2.65 billion. The deal closed in December.
  Postmates’ exploration into sidewalk delivery bots began in earnest in 2017 after the company quietly acquired Kashani’s startup Lox Inc. As head of Postmates X, the company’s R&D arm, Kashani set out to answer the question: ‘why move two-pound burritos with two-ton cars?’
 Postmates revealed its first Serve autonomous delivery bot in December 2018. A second-generation — with an identical design but different lidar sensors and few other upgrades — emerged in summer 2019 ahead of its planned commercial launch in Los Angeles.
  Instead of working with a partner, Postmates used its own delivery data to form the foundation of how it would design and deploy a sidewalk bot, according to comments Kashani made during TC Sessions: Mobility 2020 event in October.
 “When you look at the data and see that over half of deliveries are within a short distance it becomes a no brainer — these robots can actually complete them,” Kashani said at the time in reference to the application of autonomous delivery bots for delivery.
 The Postmates X used historical delivery data from the company to develop a simulation, which was then used in the design of the Serve bot. It helped the team determine what battery life would be needed and the size of the cargo hold, among other features.
 The bot only represented a sliver of Postmates’ delivery business. However, the company has seen an increase interest in the bot in Los Angeles and San Francisco — the two cities where it commercially operates — as COVID-19 fueled demand for contactless delivery.
 Kashani noted back in October that the bots had completed thousands of deliveries in Los Angeles and was preparing to expand into the city’s West Hollywood enclave. That expansion launched late last year with a twist. The Serve robots were changed to a bright pink to match the signature color of the Pink Dot stores.
title: Uber计划分拆Postmates的送货机器人手臂
title_en: Uber planning to spin out Postmates’ delivery robot arm

目前尚不清楚信息是如何被篡改的，这些信息包括药物结构示意图以及与COVID-19疫苗评估过程有关的对应关系。
   一位安全研究员卢卡斯·奥莱尼克（Lukasz Olejnik）通过Twitter引发了对泄漏的担忧，认为篡改后的数据将“完美地表达出对信任的怀疑”，因为泄漏的通信中涉及的生物技术语言将无法广泛使用。
 现在我明白了为什么这些数据非常适合播种不信任。 （虚假信息）。这些文件使用利基生物技术语言极为密封。没有门外汉能够理解这一点。非常适合共谋思维，因为它确实具有数字百分比。 https://t.co/e0qfYoV3R0
 同样，正确解析数据所需的高水平专业知识似乎也有可能通过限制其病毒感染力来限制被操纵版本可能造成的损害。
 但是值得注意的是，EMA已经对信任冠状病毒疫苗的风险提出了担忧。
 EMA在关于黑客的最新更新中写道：``在经过独立的科学评估后，12月底/ 1月初已经获得了两个针对COVID-19疫苗的欧盟市场营销授权。''
 “在欧盟感染率高的情况下，迫切需要公共卫生，以便尽快向欧盟公民提供疫苗。尽管有这样的紧迫性，但欧盟范围内一直存在共识，即不妥协高质量标准，并且不以疫苗的安全性，质量和功效等科学依据为基础提出任何建议。 
“ EMA一直在与EC以及整个网络和国际上的其他监管机构进行对话。当证据令人信服地表明接种疫苗的好处大于疫苗的任何风险时，即表示已批准。科学评估的全部详细信息可以在EMA网站上的《欧洲公共评估报告》中公开获得。”
  这次袭击尚未归因于特定的黑客团体或国家行为者，也没有确认谁负责通过在网上播下篡改的医疗文件来缝制与冠状病毒相关的虚假信息。
 但是，去年11月，微软警告说，俄罗斯和朝鲜支持的黑客把目标对准了参与COVID-19疫苗开发工作的制药公司。
 早在6月，欧盟委员会还对冠状病毒疫苗信息虚假化在未来几个月内蔓延的风险表示了担忧-同时对中国和俄罗斯进行了名字核对，称其已证实是在国家支持的针对该地区的信息虚假宣传活动背后的外国实体。
   我们之前曾见过类似的“医生泄漏”策略，这种策略可归因于俄罗斯-通常与试图通过抹黑高官候选人来干预选举有关。
 研究人员建议，负责2015-16年度违反民主党全国委员会网络的黑客将篡改过的数据窃取到泄漏的电子邮件中，这种攻击随后被归因于俄罗斯。
 虽然，最近，又出现了臭名昭著的“亨特·拜登”笔记本事件 - 这特朗普总统的支持者试图利用对他的挑战者白宫（现总统当选人）在去年的总统竞选。 
在那种情况下，在有关声称的数据缓存的发现和时间的大量可疑主张中，所有虚假信息的打击都消失了（伴随着更多的普遍认识，在政治活动中，人们对数字假涂片策略的风险有了更多的了解。俄罗斯社交媒体影响力的规模在2016年美国总统大选中出现下滑。
 从2017年开始的一次较早事件中，与法国总统伊曼纽尔·马克龙（Emmanuel Macron）竞选活动相关的电子邮件也在投票前不久在网上泄漏，这与互联网论坛上的一份文件转储相吻合，该文件表明，该总统的领先者在开曼群岛拥有一个秘密银行帐户。马克龙的政治运动声称这是假的。
 尽管在2019年，Reddit还将涉及英国大选期间平台上敏感的英美贸易谈判泄漏和放大的账户活动与涉嫌俄罗斯政治影响力活动联系在一起。
 尚不清楚泄漏的商业档案是否被篡改（已被大量编辑）。当然，它并没有给杰里米·科尔宾（Jeremy Corbyn）的工党带来压倒性选举胜利，工党在竞选活动中使用了泄漏的数据。但是，类似的，也归因于俄罗斯的较早行动涉及在多个在线平台上泄漏假文件。 （该虚假信息操作已于2019年5月被Facebook识别并撤下。）
 与COVID-19疫苗和治疗有关的篡改医疗数据的泄漏的出现，似乎是敌对的网络疾病的令人不安的演变，它们试图利用武器虚假数据为他人带来无益的结果-如果信任疫苗，将直接危害公共健康。程序被破坏。
 以前也有针对医疗数据的州级黑客，尽管没有持续进行的公共卫生紧急情况的大流行相关背景。
 例如，早在2016年，世界反兴奋剂机构确认，与俄罗斯有关的网络黑客组织“ Fancy Bear”泄露了与一些运动员的奥运会药物测试有关的机密医疗数据。在这种情况下，没有报告正在篡改数据。
note_en:
It’s not clear exactly how the information — which includes schematics of drug structures and correspondence relating to evaluation processes for COVID-19 vaccines — has been doctored.
   One security researcher, Lukasz Olejnik, who has raised concerns about the leak  via Twitter suggested the doctored data will be “perfect for sowing distrust” because the biotechnical language involved in the leaked correspondence will not be widely accessible.
 Now I understand why this data is perfect for sowing distrust. (disinformation). The documents are extremely hermetic, using niche biotech-technical language. No layperson is able to understand this. Perfect for conspiracy thinking, because it does have the numbers-percents.  https://t.co/e0qfYoV3R0
 Equally, it also seems possible that the high bar of expertise required to properly parse the data could limit how much damage the manipulated versions can do by limiting their viral appeal.
 But it’s notable the EMA has raised concerns over the risk to trust in coronavirus vaccines.
 “Two EU  marketing authorisations for COVID-19 vaccines have been granted at the end of December/beginning of January following an independent scientific assessment,” the EMA writes in the  latest update on the hack.
 “Amid the high infection rate in the EU, there is an urgent public health need to make vaccines available to EU citizens as soon as possible. Despite this urgency, there has always been consensus across the EU not to compromise the high-quality standards and to base any recommendation on the strength of the scientific evidence on a vaccine’s safety, quality and  efficacy, and nothing else.
 “EMA is in constant dialogue with the EC, and other regulators across the network and internationally. Authorisations are granted when the evidence shows convincingly that the benefits of vaccination are greater than any risks of the vaccine. Full details of the scientific assessments are publicly available in the European Public Assessment Reports on EMA’s website,” it adds.
  The attack has not been attributed to a specific hacking group or state actor and there’s no confirmation of who is responsible for trying to sew coronavirus-related disinformation by seeding doctored medical documents online.
 However, last  November Microsoft warned that hackers backed by Russia and North Korea had targeted pharmaceutical companies involved in the COVID-19 vaccine development efforts.
 Back in  June, the European Commission also raised concerns about the risks of coronavirus vaccine disinformation spreading in the coming months — simultaneously name-checking China and Russia as foreign entities it said it had confirmed as being behind state-backed disinformation campaigns targeting the region.
   We’ve seen similar ‘doctored leak’ tactics attributed to Russia before — typically related to attempts to interfere with elections by smearing candidates for high political office.
 Researchers have  suggested that the hackers responsible for the 2015-16 breaches of the Democratic National Committee’s network snuck doctored data into the leaked emails — an attack that was subsequently  attributed to Russia.
 While, more recently, there was the infamous  ‘Hunter Biden’ laptop incident — which supporters of president Trump sought to leverage against his challenger for the White House (now president-elect) in last year’s presidential race.
 In that case, any disinformation punch  fizzled out amid a raft of dubious claims around the finding and timing of the claimed data cache (along with much greater general awareness about the risk of digital fake smear tactics in political campaigns in the wake of revelations about the scale of Russia’s social media influence disops in the 2016 US presidential election).
 In an earlier incident, from  2017, emails linked to the French president Emmanuel Macron’s election campaign also leaked online shortly before the vote — coinciding with  a document dump on an Internet forum that suggested the presidential frontrunner had a secret bank account in the Cayman Islands. A claim Macron’s political movement said was fake.
 While in  2019 Reddit also linked account activity involving the leak and amplification of sensitive UK-US trade talks on its platform during the UK election campaign to a suspected Russian political influence operation.
 It’s not clear whether that leaked trade dossier had been doctored or not (it was heavily redacted). And it certainly did not deliver a landslide election win to Jeremy Corbyn’s Labour Party — which used the leaked data in its campaign. But a similar, earlier operation which was also attributed to Russia had involved the leak of fake documents on multiple online platforms. (That disinformation operation was identified and taken down by Facebook in  May 2019.)
 The emergence of leaks of doctored medical data linked to COVID-19 vaccines and treatments looks like a troubling evolution of hostile cyber disops which seek to weaponize false data to generate unhelpful outcomes for others — as there’s a direct risk to public health if trust in vaccine programs are undermined.
 There have been state level hacks targeting medical data before too — albeit without the pandemic-related backdrop of an ongoing public health emergency.
 Back in 2016, for example, the  World Anti-Doping Agency confirmed that confidential medical data related to the Olympic drug tests of a number of athletes had been leaked by the Russia-linked cyber hacking group, ‘Fancy Bear’. In that case there were no reports of the data being doctored.
title: EMA警告篡改的COVID-19疫苗数据被黑客入侵和在线泄露
title_en: EMA warns over doctored COVID-19 vaccine data hacked and leaked online

note:
马克·洛尔（Marc Lore）是一位连续企业家，他以30亿美元的价格将其创业公司Jet.com出售给了沃尔玛，然后监督了这家零售巨头在过去四年中的电子商务业务转型。他告诉了Recode。
 他的下一次创业大转变将与他目前的专业知识相去甚远：一个由“改革后的资本主义”支持的，建设“未来之城”的数十年计划。
 他嘲笑道：“这是我们将要测试的一种新的社会模式。”
 劳尔拒绝提供更多细节，但表示他准备在未来几个月内透露更多信息。听说过该项目的一些人说，重点将放在使每天的市民在城市的发展中直接获得经济上的优势。
 “设想一个城市，它拥有纽约市的活力，多样性和文化，再加上东京的效率，安全和创新以及瑞典的可持续性，治理和社会服务，”阅读该项目的愿景声明。 “这将是我们的新城市。”
 他补充说：“这将是一个终生项目。” “这是我最热衷的事情。”
 这位企业家兼高管表示，他还计划将更多的时间用于慈善事业，为初创企业提供咨询以及在上市公司董事会任职，同时还要写书并致力于电视节目的开发。他说，他也有自己想追求的新创业公司的想法，但明确表示他无意将它们作为首席执行官。 
劳尔（Lore）的离职是在沃尔玛进行了当时最大的收购后近四年半的时间。沃尔玛在2016年斥资33亿美元购买了他运营并在15个月前推出的购物网站Jet.com。
 此举对沃尔玛首席执行官道格·麦克米伦（Doug McMillon）来说是一个大胆的赌注，他这样做主要是为了雇用劳尔（Lore）和他的执行团队，希望他们能够弥合传统零售商和亚马逊之间在线销售方面的巨大差距，同时向人才领域发出信号科技行业以及对华尔街投资者而言，沃尔玛终于认真考虑要重新打造自己，以应对杰夫·贝索斯（Jeff Bezos）的科技巨头正在创造的购物的未来。
 Lore在了解沃尔玛的工作原理后来到沃尔玛，他曾与他人共同创立和经营Quidsi，这是一个以Diapers.com最著名的在线购物网站，亚马逊于2011年以5.5亿美元的价格将其收购。在离开创建Jet.com之前的几年。
 在许多方面，McMillon在Jet.com和Lore上的赌注都是成功的。研究公司eMarketer称，沃尔玛现在是仅次于美国亚马逊的第二大在线购物网站，其在线销售的市场份额在Lore任职期间翻了一番以上，达到5.8％。自收购以来，该零售商的股价已上涨80％以上，高于同期标准普尔500指数的涨幅。沃尔玛现在的价值超过4,000亿美元，在商业世界中作为数字恐龙的声誉已大大降低。
 洛尔说：“我想人们对沃尔玛的看法与四年半前有所不同。”
 在Lore的领导下，沃尔玛还将其在线商品目录扩大了八倍，同时为在亚马逊上销售的小商户提供了另一个市场选择，以尝试使其业务多元化。沃尔玛还推出了免费的两天送货服务，以及部分商品的第二天和当日发货选项。
 9月，它推出了Walmart +，这是一项每年98美元的会员计划，以杂货店送货和其他津贴为特色，以替代Amazon Prime。沃尔玛的电子商务销售额在2020年第三季度同比增长了近80％，因为在Covid-19大流行期间，沃尔玛继续是零售行业最大的消费者购物趋势受益者之一。 
不过，情况并非一帆风顺。亚马逊在美国的电子商务业务仍然是沃尔玛业务的近七倍，而洛尔部门的头几年的巨额支出和严重的财务损失在内部造成了压力。
 Recode先前曾报道说，沃尔玛规模更大，利润更高的实体业务的前任领导人对Lore的一些内部投资和企业家的信任为沃尔玛的路边皮卡杂货店业务的发展赢得了声誉，这在技术上是在线购物产品，但由商店员工执行。内部没有上市的初创公司包括Jetblack，这是针对富裕城市居民的礼宾服务。劳尔之前曾说过，他了解这种挫败感，并称紧张局势“并非不健康”。
 但是亏损导致沃尔玛领导层控制了一些Lore和团队的雄心勃勃的计划，包括大量游说为与亚马逊更好地竞争而建设的新在线购物仓库，以及建立数字化本地产品组合的激进收购战略可以帮助零售商将商品目录与亚马逊区分开的消费品牌，并吸引新的客户群体。
 沃尔玛确实收购了Lore旗下的一些在线本地数字品牌，例如Bonobos，ModCloth和Eloquii，但这位高管最初计划进行更多交易。沃尔玛最终出售了ModCloth，并一度也考虑出售Bonobos。
 洛尔（Lore）周四表示：“这是我无法实现的领域之一。” “那些公司没有像我希望的那样表现。”
 由于对企业家的热爱，Lore的离职预计在某个时候发生，而这更是何时而不是是否存在的问题。他通过购买喷气机获得的巨额薪酬要求他在沃尔玛呆了异常长的五年-直到2021年9月-才能获得全部付款，其中包括仅在交易的最后一年进行的股票分配，目前的价值约为150美元。百万。
 他说，双方仍在努力解决他离职的财务问题，并且对他早早退出感到满意，因为该公司去年将其大部分电子商务和实体店部门合并为沃尔玛美国首席执行官约翰·弗纳（John Furner）领导的统一团队。 
洛尔（Lore）仍将是沃尔玛（Walmart）的顾问，并表示，他与首席执行官麦克米伦（McMillon）的密切关系意味着他将竭尽所能来帮助这家零售巨头-但在线购物失败者-将来获得成功。 这位58岁的零售业巨头需要做什么来弥合与亚马逊的鸿沟？  洛尔说：“继续大胆而不是追随者。” “快速跟进策略将无法完成。”  每天在Vox，我们的目标是回答您最重要的问题，并为您和我们的全球受众提供信息，以帮助您理解。 Vox的工作比以往任何时候都吸引了更多人，但是我们独特的解释性新闻品牌需要资源。 您的财政捐款不会构成捐赠，但是它将使我们的员工能够继续向所有需要的人提供免费的文章，视频和播客。 请考虑立即向Vox捐款，最低$ 3。
note_en:
Marc Lore, a serial entrepreneur who  sold his startup Jet.com to Walmart for $3 billion and then oversaw the transformation of the retail giant’s e-commerce business over the last four years, is leaving his full-time role with the company at the end of the month, he told Recode.
 His next big entrepreneurial swing will be something far afield from his current expertise: a multi-decade project to build “a city of the future” supported by “a reformed version of capitalism.”
 “It’s a new model for society we’ll be testing,” he teased.
 Lore declined to offer more details, but said he would be prepared to reveal additional information in the coming months. Some who have heard of the project say one focus will be on giving everyday citizens direct economic upside in the city’s growth.
 “Imagine a city with the vibrancy, diversity and culture of New York City combined with the efficiency, safety and innovation of Tokyo and the sustainability, governance, and social services of Sweden,” reads the vision statement for the project. “This will be our New City.”
 “This is going to be a lifelong project,” he added. “It’s the thing I’m most passionate about.”
 The entrepreneur and executive said he also plans to spend more time on philanthropy, advising startups, and serving on public company boards, while also writing a book and working on developing a TV show. He said he has ideas for new startups he wanted to pursue as well, but made clear he had no intention of running them as CEO.
 Lore’s departure comes nearly four and a half years after Walmart made its then-biggest acquisition, spending $3.3 billion in 2016 to purchase the shopping website Jet.com that he ran and launched just 15 months earlier.
 The move was a bold bet for Walmart CEO Doug McMillon, who did so mainly to hire Lore and his executive team, in the hopes that they could close the enormous gap in online sales between the legacy retailer and Amazon, while signaling to talent in the technology industry — and to Wall Street investors — that Walmart was finally serious about reinventing itself for the future of shopping that Jeff Bezos’s tech giant was creating.
 Lore arrived at Walmart with inside knowledge of how Amazon worked — he previously co-founded and ran Quidsi, a collection of online shopping sites best known for Diapers.com, which Amazon acquired for around $550 million in 2011. Lore worked at Amazon for a few years before leaving to create Jet.com.
 In many ways, McMillon’s bet on Jet.com and Lore was a success. Walmart.com is now the No. 2 online shopping site after Amazon in the US, more than doubling its market share of online sales to 5.8 percent during Lore’s tenure, according to the research firm eMarketer. The retailer’s stock price has increased more than 80 percent since the acquisition — which is greater than the growth of the S&P 500 during the same period. Walmart is now valued at more than $400 billion and has shed much of its reputation as a digital dinosaur in the business world.
 “I’d like to think people think about Walmart a bit differently than they did four and a half years ago,” Lore said.
 Under Lore, Walmart has also expanded its online merchandise catalog eightfold, while giving small merchants selling on Amazon another marketplace option to try to diversify their businesses. Walmart also introduced free two-day shipping as well as next-day and same-day delivery options on a limited selection of goods.
 In September, it unveiled Walmart+, a $98-a-year membership program featuring grocery delivery and other perks, as an alternative to Amazon Prime. Walmart’s e-commerce sales rose nearly 80 percent year over year in the third quarter of 2020, as it continued to be one of the retail industry’s biggest beneficiaries of consumer shopping trends during the Covid-19 pandemic.
 It has not all been smooth, though. Amazon’s US e-commerce business is still nearly seven times larger than Walmart’s, and Lore’s division’s heavy spending and steep financial losses in the first couple of years created tension internally.
 Recode previously reported that the former leader of Walmart’s much larger, and profitable, brick-and-mortar business  bristled over some of Lore’s internal investments and the credit the entrepreneur garnered for the growth of Walmart’s curbside pickup grocery business, which was technically an online shopping product but executed by store employees. Among the internal startups that didn’t pan out was  Jetblack, a concierge service targeting wealthy city dwellers. Lore has previously said he understood the frustration and  called the tension “not unhealthy.”
 But the losses led Walmart leadership to rein in some of Lore and the team’s more ambitious plans, including a massive buildout of new online shopping warehouses they lobbied for to better compete with Amazon, and  an aggressive acquisition strategy to build a portfolio of digital-native consumer brands that could help the retailer differentiate its merchandise catalog from Amazon and attract new customer demographics.
 Walmart did acquire a few online-native digital brands like Bonobos, ModCloth, and Eloquii under Lore, but the executive initially planned to make more deals. Walmart ended up selling ModCloth and at one time considered a sale of Bonobos, too.
 “It’s one of the areas that didn’t play out the way I would have hoped,” Lore said on Thursday. “Those businesses didn’t perform the way I hoped they would.”
 Lore’s departure was expected at some point due to his entrepreneurial leanings, and it was more a question of  when than if. His massive compensation package from the Jet acquisition  required him to remain at Walmart for an unusually long five years — until September 2021 — to receive his full payout, which included a stock allocation in the last year of the deal alone that’s currently valued at around $150 million.
 He said the two sides were still working through the financial implications of his departure, and were comfortable with his slightly early exit because the company last year combined much of its e-commerce and physical store divisions into unified teams under Walmart US CEO John Furner.
 Lore will remain an adviser to Walmart and said his close relationship with McMillon, the CEO, means he will do anything he can to help the retail giant — but online shopping underdog — succeed in the future. What does the 58-year-old retail titan need to do to close the gap with Amazon?
 “To continue to be bold and not be a follower,” Lore said. “The fast-follower strategy is not going to get it done.”

 Dubstep的歌曲通常被批评为听起来非常计算机生成，并且往往过于激进/“数字化”，以致于很多人无法欣赏。人们开玩笑说他们听起来好像有人在增加现代噪音的过程中增加了低音和鼓声并不少见。
 对于某些曲目，这比其他更真实。毕竟，这是一种类型，具有更激进的解释和更轻松的解释。
 但这让我思考，将机器可读数据实际嵌入到dubstep轨道中要花多大的精力，同时还要确保人类也能欣赏到声音……
 让我们来跟踪一下，这是Skrillex的构建和完善-正确的方法：
   如果您可以忍受，那是个好消息！您可能会满意以下所有内容。
 上面是嵌入的歌曲的频谱图，因此您可以更好地了解歌曲中所有频段的状况。 
如图所示，有很多频谱可以隐藏数据，但是它存在于高频段，音频压缩可能会将其删除。 那么，如果我们在低音线的频段上看起来较低，该怎么办？  这是0-100hz的声音（您有更多机会在头戴式/耳塞式手机上收听）：  如果我们回到原始位置并移除100hz频段及以下频段，听起来像是：  $ sox orig.wav onlyhigher.wav sinc -b 0 0.2k-22k $ cp onlyhigher.wav tmp.wav $＃做第二轮以确保下带确实为空$ sox tmp.wav onlyhigher.wav sinc -b 0 0.2k-22k $ rm tmp.wav  注意：如果您调高音量以收听最后一个音量，则需要立即还原。  如果您像我一样，并且数学背景很差，那么每次您看到这样的事情：  不幸的是，这几乎完全是DSP世界。 但是，考虑到我在此过程中遇到的困难，我将尝试描述没有任何花哨符号的情况。 
首先，这基本上是最基本的形式。向扬声器发出信号，告知扬声器应处于什么位置。范围从1（始终）到-1（始终）。在这些位置上移动会排空空气并发出声音！
  我的想法是，如果像这样将波形移动到上端：
    这意味着我们现在可以观察到两种状态！最好的部分是（至少我的）人耳看不到这种区别。

 该解码器的工作原理是计算“帧”中有多少个样本为负数，并以此为基础声明是1位还是0位。

note_en:
[Warning: Those who can’t stand EDM/dubstep, oh boy do I have bad news for you in regards to this blog post]
 Dubstep songs are often criticized as sounding extremely computer generated and often just too aggressive/“digital” for a lot of people to enjoy. It’s not uncommon for people to joke that they sound like someone had added a bassline and drums to  modem noises
 For some tracks this is truer than others. After all, it’s a genre with more aggressive interpretations and more relaxed ones.
 But that had me thinking, how much effort would it be to actually embed machine readable data inside a dubstep track, while ensuring that the sound could be enjoyed by humans as well…
 Let’s take a track to work with, this is the buildup and drop of  Skrillex - Right In:
   If can you tolerate listening to that, Good news! You will likely be fine with everything below.
 Above here is a  spectrogram of the song that was embedded, so you have a better idea of what is going on with all the frequency bands in the song.
 As shown, there is plenty of spectrum to hide data in, but it exists on the high bands where audio compression may remove it. So, what if we looked lower in the frequency bands, at the bassline?
 Here is what 0-100hz sounds like (you have a better chance listening to this on head/ear phones):
    If we go back to the original and remove the 100hz bands and below, it sounds like:
 $ sox orig.wav onlyhigher.wav sinc -b 0 0.2k-22k$ cp onlyhigher.wav tmp.wav$ # Do a 2nd round to ensure the lower band is really empty$ sox tmp.wav onlyhigher.wav sinc -b 0 0.2k-22k$ rm tmp.wav
  Note: If you turned up volume to hear the last one, you will want to revert that now.
   If you are like me and have a pretty poor maths background then every time you see things like this:
   Unfortunately, this is pretty much entirely the  DSP world. However given that I struggled through this, I will try and describe what I’m doing without any fancy symbols.
 To start, this is basically sound in its most basic form; a signal to a speaker telling it what position it should be in. Ranging from 1 (all the way out) to -1 (all the way in). Moving over these positions displaces air and makes sound!
  My thought was, if we move the wave form to the upper end like so:
    This means we now have two states we can observe! The best part of this is that the difference is not noticeable to (at least my) human ears.
  package mainimport (	"encoding/binary"	"flag"	"log"	"os")func main() {	ins := flag.String("input", "./in.f64.data", "")	outs := flag.String("out", "./out.f64.data", "")	flag.Parse()	inf, err := os.OpenFile(*ins, os.O_RDONLY, 0644)	if err != nil {		log.Fatalf("Unable to open output file %s", err.Error())	}	outf, err := os.OpenFile(*outs, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, 0644)	if err != nil {		log.Fatalf("Unable to open output file %s", err.Error())	}	Symbolrate := 11000	SamplesUntilChange := Symbolrate	UpperFlip := false	bits := 0	for {		var raws float64		err := binary.Read(inf, binary.LittleEndian, &raws)		if err != nil {			log.Printf("Leaving %s", err.Error())			break		}		// First, obtain a upper flip		normie := (raws + 1) / 2		SamplesUntilChange--		if SamplesUntilChange == 0 {			UpperFlip = !UpperFlip			SamplesUntilChange = Symbolrate			bits++		}		if !UpperFlip {			normie = normie * -1		}		if SamplesUntilChange < 1000 {			dest := normie * -1			normie = lerp(dest, normie, float64(SamplesUntilChange)/1000.0)		}		binary.Write(outf, binary.LittleEndian, normie)	}	log.Printf("Finished with %d bits / %d bytes", bits, bits/8)}func lerp(a, b, n float64) float64 {	return (1-n)*a + n*b}
 At this point we can reassemble the output bassline file and the upper frequencies, and see if it is playable:
 $ sox orig.wav onlylower.wav sinc 0k-0.1k$ sox orig.wav onlyhigher.wav sinc 0.1k-22k$ ffmpeg -i onlylower.wav -f f64le -ar 44100 -ac 1 -y in.f64.data$ ./ASK-dubstep -input in.f64.data$ ffmpeg -f f64le -ar 44100 -ac 1 -i out.f64.data -y encoded-bass.wav$ sox -m encoded-bass.wav onlyhigher.wav encoded-bassline.wav
     I might be wrong, but as far as I can tell, this is a form of  Amplitude-shift keying.
 To make it encode our own data, we can use a simple library to help us read bits in a nice easy to use way, and then include them in the audio:
 package mainimport (	"encoding/binary"	"flag"	"log"	"os"	"strings"	"github.com/dgryski/go-bitstream")func main() {	ins := flag.String("input", "./in.f64.data", "")	outs := flag.String("out", "./out.f64.data", "")	encodetarget := flag.String("data", "Hello World!", "")	flag.Parse()	inf, err := os.OpenFile(*ins, os.O_RDONLY, 0644)	if err != nil {		log.Fatalf("Unable to open output file %s", err.Error())	}	outf, err := os.OpenFile(*outs, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, 0644)	if err != nil {		log.Fatalf("Unable to open output file %s", err.Error())	}	sr := strings.NewReader(*encodetarget)	bitreader := bitstream.NewReader(sr)	Symbolrate := 5500	SamplesUntilChange := Symbolrate	UpperFlip := false	bits := 0	nextbit := false	for {		var raws float64		err := binary.Read(inf, binary.LittleEndian, &raws)		if err != nil {			log.Printf("Leaving %s", err.Error())			break		}		// First, obtain a upper flip		normie := (raws + 1) / 2		SamplesUntilChange--		if SamplesUntilChange == 0 {			UpperFlip = nextbit			b, _ := bitreader.ReadBit()			nextbit = bool(b)			SamplesUntilChange = Symbolrate			bits++		}		if !UpperFlip {			normie = normie * -1		}		if SamplesUntilChange < 1000 && UpperFlip != nextbit {			dest := normie * -1			normie = lerp(dest, normie, float64(SamplesUntilChange)/1000.0)		}		binary.Write(outf, binary.LittleEndian, normie)	}	log.Printf("Finished with %d bits / %d bytes", bits, bits/8)}func lerp(a, b, n float64) float64 {	return (1-n)*a + n*b}
  package mainimport (	"encoding/binary"	"flag"	"fmt"	"log"	"os"	bitstream "github.com/dgryski/go-bitstream")func main() {	ins := flag.String("input", "./in.f64.data", "")	symbolrate := flag.Int("srate", 5500, "Symbol rate in samples")	flag.Parse()	inf, err := os.OpenFile(*ins, os.O_RDONLY, 0644)	if err != nil {		log.Fatalf("Unable to open output file %s", err.Error())	}	bw := bitstream.NewWriter(os.Stdout)	bw.WriteBit(bitstream.Bit(false))	SamplesUntilChange := *symbolrate	bits := 1	negscore := 0	for {		var raws float64		err := binary.Read(inf, binary.LittleEndian, &raws)		if err != nil {			fmt.Print("\n")			log.Printf("Leaving %s", err.Error())			break		}		isNeg := raws < 0		if isNeg {			negscore++		}		SamplesUntilChange--		if SamplesUntilChange == 0 {			rsp := negscore < (*symbolrate / 2)			if bits != 1 {				bw.WriteBit(bitstream.Bit(rsp))			}			negscore = 0			SamplesUntilChange = *symbolrate			bits++		}	}	log.Printf("Finished with %d bits / %d bytes", bits, bits/8)}
 This decoder works by counting how many samples in a “frame” are negative, and based on that declaring if it’s a 1 or a 0 bit.
   To finish off, here is a live demo of it all working with a different song ( Smooth - Nowhere):
  I have pushed the code on github as always (though it’s not really in a usable state):  https://github.com/benjojo/dubstep-data
 And if you enjoyed this, you will be glad to know that I am going to be at  Recurse Center in NY for the next 10 weeks! Meaning you can follow my  Twitter or  RSS to keep up with the other silly things I will do!

Mattermost is an open source, private cloud slackalternative written in Go and React. It has many integrations and cancan connect to mysql or postgres for persistence. While there are many"hello world" tutorials out there for running the Nanos unikernel wethought this would be a nice little example to showcase two things:
 1) How to run a multi-node setup. Most web applications are more thanjust one actual application - the canonical example would be a webappserver and its corresponding database.
 2) How to interact with taps and bridges for a faster networkingexperience or if you wanted to build your own private cloud. (Creating taps,today, will only work on Linux as the tuntaposx package does not workanymore but we're looking at alternatives.)
     We start by running the mysql server as a unikernel.We've created a convenient package to use and the only thing you need todo is set your base volume size. You could create an external volume butwe'll leave that to a different article.

    Ops load by default will re-build the image each time so if you wantto skip that you can pass the '-s' flag or use 'ops instance create' instead.
   For mattermost we'll want to edit the mattermost config.json (not tobe confused with the config.json we use for ops). Create a 'config'directory to store it in. Copy the config.json in. If you want, you canuntar the tarball found in ~/.ops/packages/mattermost_5.12.0.tar.gz and grab acopy there. Then you'll want to edit the mysql "DataSource" line inconfig.json to modify the ip address and l/p:
  Obviously this is just for demo purposes but as a side note - if youdo accidently deploy this to a public ip with default credentials thebots that scour the internet looking for these things will be able toconnect and write all the SQL their little bot hearts desire, but theirdb encryption operations fail, the cryptojackingfails, and all the C&C stuff fails. It just shows you how interestingthis style of architecture truly is.
 Now let's load up mattermost and give it it's own tap and assign it a static ip address:
